6		"os/exec"

8	)

10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

24	// runExecutionCleanupPass conservatively runs the shared cleanup manager under
25	// a project-level lock. When another process already owns the lock, the pass
26	// is skipped and observed rather than blocking concurrent deletion.
27	func runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, runner executionCleanupRunner, log io.Writer, emit func(string, map[string]any), reason string) (ExecutionCleanupSummary, bool, error) {
28		if runner == nil {
29			return ExecutionCleanupSummary{}, false, nil
30		}
31		if projectRoot == "" {
32			return ExecutionCleanupSummary{}, false, fmt.Errorf("execution cleanup: project root is empty")
33		}
34	
35		release, acquired, err := tryAcquireExecutionCleanupLock(projectRoot)
36		if err != nil {
37			return ExecutionCleanupSummary{}, false, err
38		}
39		if !acquired {
40			if emit != nil {
41				emit("cleanup.skipped", map[string]any{
42					"project_root": projectRoot,
43					"reason":       reason,
44					"lock_path":    executionCleanupLockPath(projectRoot),
45				})
46			}
47			return ExecutionCleanupSummary{}, true, nil
48		}
49		defer release()

feat(cleanup): report stale attempt descendant processes [ddx-47d9100a]

Add a stale attempt process scanner to ExecutionCleanupManager that
reports (and optionally kills) processes whose cwd is under the DDx
execution temp root. Detection uses ownership evidence (cwd path),
not command-name matching, so non-harness descendants (sh, cargo,
test servers) are covered. Linux implementation reads /proc; other
platforms report ProcessCleanupUnavailable=true. Dry-run records
findings with would_kill=true without invoking the killer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1

48			}, true
49		case in.Budget:

51		case in.NoProgress:

53		case in.Blocked:
54			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
55		case in.Once:
56			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
57		case in.NoReadyWork && in.Mode != executeloop.ModeWatch:
58			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "drained"}, true
59		default:
60			return StopDecision{}, false
61		}
62	}

70		default:
